title: festuca pratensis var. subspicata
intro: Festuca pratensis var. subspicata, also known as a subspecies of meadow fescue, is a perennial grass native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its slender stems and feathery leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, due to the presence of sesquiterpene compounds. The health benefits of Festuca pratensis var. subspicata are said to include the reduction of inflammation, improvement of respiratory health, and the promotion of wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
